mission.title: podman-container-tools: Mac: current HEAD errors out on `--add-host` using `host-gateway` with "host containers internal IP address is empty"
mission.description: Mac: current HEAD errors out on `--add-host` using `host-gateway` with "host containers internal IP address is empty". This issue affects 21+ users.

steps.title: Identify podman-container-tools troubleshoot symptoms
steps.description:
Check for the issue in your podman-container-tools installation:
```bash
podman --version
podman info --format json | jq .version
```
Look for errors or warnings that may indicate the issue.

steps.title: Review podman-container-tools configuration
steps.description:
Review the relevant podman-container-tools configuration:
### Issue Description

With current HEAD (Git hash bbd4476), an error occurs when using the `--add-host` option with `host-gateway` special IP designator, even though this is supposed to be supported with

steps.title: Apply the fix for Mac: current HEAD errors out on `--add-host` using…
steps.description:
> I found a workaround so I can handle both docker and podman users:
> 
> ```
> host_gateway=$(podman network inspect -f "{{range .Subnets}}{{.Gateway}}{{end}}" podman 2>/dev/null || echo "host-gateway")
> 
> docker run --add-host foobar:"$host_gateway" -it busybox
> ```

This returns the ip of the
```yaml
podman run --add-host foobar:host-gateway -it busybox
Error: preparing container a90605b73f90a2cab9db7142643a111f010ebabc6d8a74eb0bfeda0f525f97cf for attach: failed to create new hosts file: unable to replace "host-gateway" of host entry "foobar:host-gateway": host containers internal IP address is empty
```

steps.title: Confirm Mac: current HEAD errors out on `--add-host`… is resolved
steps.description:
Verify the fix by checking that the original error no longer occurs:
Test podman-container-tools to confirm the issue is resolved.
Confirm that the issue symptoms are gone.

resolution.summary:
> I found a workaround so I can handle both docker and podman users:
> 
> ```
> host_gateway=$(podman network inspect -f "{{range .Subnets}}{{.Gateway}}{{end}}" podman 2>/dev/null || echo "host-gateway")
> 
> docker run --add-host foobar:"$host_gateway" -it busybox
> ```

This returns the ip of the subnet gateway which is not the host ip for podman machine nor would this ip makes sense for
codeSnippets:
podman run --add-host foobar:host-gateway -it busybox
Error: preparing container a90605b73f90a2cab9db7142643a111f010ebabc6d8a74eb0bfeda0f525f97cf for attach: failed to create new hosts file: unable to replace "host-gateway" of host entry "foobar:host-gateway": host containers internal IP address is empty

(installed using `brew install podman --HEAD`, resulting in Git hash bbd4476)
